macro_summary: Thursday's session was a tug-of-war between hot inflation and easing oil: the Fed's preferred PCE gauge hit a 3-year high at 4.1% headline / 3.4% core, reinforcing Chair Warsh's hawkish hold, yet the 10-year Treasury yield still fell 1bp to 4.39% as WTI slid another 1.1% to $70.69 — fully unwinding the Hormuz risk premium. Tech bore the brunt, with the Nasdaq dropping 1.1% while Micron's post-close Q3 blowout (EPS $25.11, +21% beat, $22B supply commitments) lifted the SOXX 3.9% — though SOXX data carries a 5.3% price-source conflict, so treat the magnitude cautiously. The rate-hike-versus-oil-disinflation cross-current left the S&P 500 down 0.4%.
commodities_note: WTI at $70.69 (-1.1% Thursday, -7.7% for the week) has fully surrendered the Hormuz war premium as tanker traffic through the strait hit its highest since the February 28 conflict began. Gold continues to bleed, down 0.3% to $4,026 (-9.5% over the month) as real yields and a firm dollar sap the bid. DXY flat at 101.39. VIX at 18.89 remains unnervingly calm given the inflation print.

Four consecutive beats with accelerating trajectory (EPS 4.78 → 12.2 → 25.11) confirm the HBM cycle is structural, not cyclical. Forward PE of 8.2x on 196% revenue growth is absurdly cheap — a 73% discount to the semis cluster. But at +53.9% above its 50-day SMA, the SMA50 warning is active: a pullback to the 50-day would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support at $854 as the re-entry reference. No entry until consolidation brings price closer to a tradeable level.

writeup.headline: Downgraded to CAUTION — Cerebras -30% in a month, IPO hype fully unwound, RSI 30.9 oversold.
writeup.what_to_do: Cerebras is in freefall — -10.3% Thursday, -21.1% this week, -30.3% over the month. The Q1 print (revenue ~$193M, narrower net loss of ~$14M per Simply Wall St., Jun 25) was overshadowed by lower gross-margin guidance and the capital-intensive shift to an AI cloud model. At 176x forward PE with a 43% hardware gross margin (per IA analysis), the valuation is punishing. R:R of 5.0:1 is mechanically attractive but derived from a single resistance zone at $253.52 — treat with skepticism. The WSE-3 architectural ceilings (I/O bandwidth, no FP8 support per IA) cap the bull case. Avoid until price stabilises and the margin trajectory clarifies — this is a falling knife, not a dip.

writeup.headline: Upgraded to ACCUMULATE — all 8 mechanical gates pass, R:R 2.5:1, wide-stop 1.53:1.
writeup.what_to_do: All eight ACCUMULATE gates pass mechanically. R:R of 2.5:1 to $15.19 using the 50-day SMA stop at $13.49, and wide-stop R:R to structural support at $13.18 is 1.53:1 — clears the 1.5 gate. Three independent support legs: (1) NVDA Spectrum-X backend networking, (2) Google Cloud Gemini AI integration (per Simply Wall St., Jun 25), and (3) Finnish counter-drone consortium (per GuruFocus, Jun 25) adding defense diversification. Entry zone $13.80–14.00, invalidation at $13.49 (50-day SMA). Forward PE of 28.7x on 2.4% revenue growth is rich, but the AI networking catalyst is early-stage — half position. ACCUMULATE — starter position. Fragility: slow-cycle compounder — single-quarter swings should not drive thesis changes; Nokia reports in 27 days (Jul 23), size accordingly. [paper]

writeup.headline: BE -10.4% profit-taking, RCP consolidation session 15 of 25 — Oracle catalyst still gating.
writeup.what_to_do: Data anomaly: fast_info prev_close $344.86 vs history $326.19 diverge by 5.1% — using the history close as canonical. The Oracle 'Project Jupiter' deal (the structural AI-power catalyst behind the gap) drove an +11.2% gap on Jun 3; Bloom is now in session 15 of the 25-session consolidation gate. Path A (SMA50 retest) triggered at session 6. Graduation requires either Path A completion or Path B breakout above ~$293 (8%-band). Failure triggers: 5+ closes below $273 (the gap-settle less 5%) and below the post-gap SMA50, or 3+ closes below $302.85 (pre-gap close) within 60 sessions. Thursday's -10.4% drop is profit-taking after a sector-wide fuel-cell reversal (per 24/7 Wall St., Jun 25). No entry until graduation. Fragility: Oracle concentration — Project Jupiter is the de-risking catalyst but creates customer concentration.

The SiC power-semiconductor datacenter pivot thesis is intact — analysts lifted fair value estimates to €77.67 on AI power demand conviction (per Simply Wall St., Jun 24). But China's Supreme Court upheld an injunction blocking Infineon's GaN products in mainland China (per Simply Wall St., Jun 24), a tangible revenue headwind. PEG of 0.76 and forward PE of 31.4x make this fundamentally attractive, but the entry requires a pullback toward the 50-day at €68.97. No entry until then.

writeup.headline: PLTR -21.5% in a month, RSI 27.4 oversold — R:R 2.7:1, wide-stop 1.34:1 below the gate.
writeup.what_to_do: Palantir hit a 12-month low on Thursday (per Barrons, Jun 25) — seven straight losing sessions. R:R of 2.7:1 to $136.10 is mechanically above 2.0, but wide-stop R:R to structural support at $85.82 is 1.34:1 — below the 1.5 ACCUMULATE gate. EPS trajectory is accelerating (0.21 → 0.25 → 0.33 over 3 quarters, latest beat +17.9%) and the defense-AI thesis has real contract momentum ($10B Army IDIQ framework, $448M Navy ShipOS per DoD). But at 51.5x forward PE (122% premium to BigTech cluster), the multiple is punitive in a hawkish-rate regime. Reassess on a volume-climax reversal or after Q2 earnings in 39 days confirm the 2026 $7.18-7.20B guide. Fragility: defense-contract dependency — IDIQ ceiling is not firm revenue.

writeup.headline: SiTime sizing R:R 5.2:1 at the 50-day — PE 62x, elevated; thesis interesting, not actionable.
writeup.what_to_do: SiTime has pulled back to just +1.1% above its 50-day SMA — technically in the entry zone. Headline R:R of 12.6:1 is distorted by a 1.1% stop; the sizing R:R to structural support at $657.38 is 5.2:1 — genuinely compelling. Eight straight quarters of triple-digit profit growth (per Investor's Business Daily, Jun 25) and the MEMS-timing displacement of quartz in AI networking is a structural thesis. But forward PE of 62x with PEG of 3.82 is expensive — doesn't clear the on_deck valuation gate. The stock is a leveraged NVDA play for risk-off contagion. Avoid for now; reassess when PE compresses below 40x or a named hyperscaler design win surfaces. Tight_upside: the entry only activates on a confirmed SMA50 hold with volume.

singapore.summary: All three banks remain extended 5–7% above their 50-day SMAs with declining volume and R:R ratios below 1:1 — no entry in the cluster. The NIM-compression headwind is unchanged: US10Y at 4.39% still sits above the 4.20% threshold, and the MAS S$NEER appreciation stance holds until at least the October review. UOB is the cluster outlier on valuation at 10.9x forward PE (21% discount to the cluster median) but the macro block is cluster-wide, not ticker-specific.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally compressed — NIM relief gated on US10Y breaking below 4.20%
singapore.key_development: OCBC and DBS extension eased slightly on Thursday's pullback but volume was anemic across the cluster — no accumulation signal.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is split into two distinct camps: the HBM/memory names (MU +53.9%, 000660.KS +37.9%) are earnings-driven extended and blocked, while the AI-compute names (NVDA -6.8% below SMA50, AVGO -8.0%) are consolidating toward potentially tradeable entries. NVDA is the cluster standout — forward PE 15.4x (49% discount to cluster median) with sizing R:R of 3.0:1, the most actionable setup in the group once the tight upside at $197.39 breaks. SOXX jumped 3.9% on Micron's blowout but has a 5.3% price-source conflict — treat the magnitude with caution.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically bifurcated — memory extended, compute consolidating
semiconductors.key_development: Micron's Q3 blowout (EPS $25.11, $22B supply commitments per Reuters) is the single most important semis datapoint this week — it confirms memory tightness through 2027 and validates the HBM thesis for MU and 000660.KS, but both are too extended to enter.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is under severe pressure — MSFT is having its worst month since 2000 (-35% YTD), AMZN has missed the AI rally entirely (-14.4% monthly), and PLTR hit a 12-month low. Only GOOG is showing relative resilience, flat on Thursday. The common thread is capex anxiety: the market is now discounting the $850B hyperscaler spend (per NY Post) as an expense burden rather than a growth driver. MSFT's RSI at 28.8 and PLTR's at 27.4 are deeply oversold, but the R:R math doesn't yet support entry — the cluster needs a sentiment catalyst (earnings, capex ROI data) to reverse the rout.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — capex-revenue circularity concern is the dominant narrative
big_tech.key_development: PCE inflation at 3-year high (4.1% headline, 3.4% core) reinforces the hawkish-rate headwind for capex-heavy BigTech — duration-sensitive names remain in the penalty box.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received NVDA's matching $2B photonics investment alongside LITE — shares the co-packaged optics AI datacenter thesis. Coherent's 6-inch InP yield struggles (per IA analysis) make LITE's Greensboro fab the production moat, but COHR remains the direct read-through on any photonics catalyst day.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's Aug 13 earnings confirm CPO revenue acceleration above $500M quarterly run-rate, signaling the photonics ecosystem is scaling.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking switch leader — directly benefits from the same hyperscaler capex cycle driving NOK's Spectrum-X and 800G optical rollouts. Arista is the merchant-switch counterpart to NVDA's InfiniBand ecosystem.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after NVDA reclaims $200 with volume — Arista typically leads networking names in an AI-infrastructure recovery.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Samsung's $648B 10-year South Korea investment plan (per Reuters, Jun 26) includes $194B for chip factories, directly competing with SK Hynix (000660.KS) and Micron (MU) in the HBM supply chain. Samsung's HBM3E qualification struggles have tightened the duopoly, but this capex signals they intend to close the gap.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung's HBM3E qualification is confirmed by NVDA — that breaks the SK Hynix/Micron duopoly and re-rates Samsung's memory division.

active_risks: Hormuz de-escalation progressing — tanker traffic at highest since Feb 28 conflict, but Iran still asserts control over non-designated routes and a cargo vessel was hit near Oman Thursday (per CNBC, Jun 26). WTI fully unwound war premium at $70.69.

US-China tech decoupling deepening — Alibaba sues US Defense Department over military-company designation (per Morning Journal, Jun 24); Dutch trade minister opposes MATCH Act targeting ASML (per TechCrunch, Jun 24). China retaliatory export controls on US defense firms remain in place.

Fed hawkish hold reinforced — May PCE hit 3-year high at 4.1% headline / 3.4% core (per Yahoo Finance, Jun 25); BofA's 75bps rate-hike call under Chair Warsh persists. Next catalyst: July 2 Non-Farm Payrolls.

Ukraine-Russia grinding attrition — Ukrainian forces systematically degrading Russian logistics in southern occupied territories (per The Counteroffensive, Jun 25); French Navy seized another Russian shadow-fleet tanker (per Marine News, Jun 25). No market-moving escalation.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $70.69 has fully unwound the Hormuz risk premium — the defense-name bid (PLTR, BE) and energy-cost anxiety that dominated Q2 are fading. The PCE print at 4.1% is the dominant macro signal: it cements Warsh's hawkish hold and keeps duration-sensitive BigTech (MSFT -35% YTD, AMZN -14.4% monthly) under pressure. The semis cluster is bifurcated — Micron's blowout lifts memory names but the rate backdrop caps multiple expansion for the compute names (NVDA, AVGO).
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: PCE inflation hit 3-year high at 4.1% headline (per Yahoo Finance, Jun 25) — the most hawkish inflation print since Warsh took the Fed chair, reinforcing rate-hike speculation. Cargo vessel hit near Oman Thursday (per CNBC) — a reminder that Hormuz remains fragile despite reopening.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, WTI drifts toward $65–70 as traffic normalises. PCE stays elevated near 4%, Fed holds through 2026 with one hike penciled. Semis cluster stays bifurcated: memory names (MU, 000660.KS) consolidate from extreme extension, compute names (NVDA, AVGO) build bases near their SMA50s. BigTech (MSFT, AMZN) remains range-bound awaiting Q2 earnings for capex-ROI validation.

optimistic.description: Hormuz fully normalises, WTI drops to $60–65, removing the energy-cost headwind for hyperscalers. June CPI (Jul 15) surprises lower, breaking the inflation momentum. Fed pauses rate-hike talk. NVDA reclaims $200+ with volume, semis cluster re-rates, and MSFT/AMZN bounce on easing capex-anxiety. PLTR defense contracts convert to firm revenue, validating the >$7B guide.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz re-escalates — Iran closes the strait again after a provocation, WTI surges back to $85+. July 2 NFP prints hot, cementing a July rate hike. BigTech sells off further as capex costs spike with rates; SOXX drops 10%+ on the double-hit of energy costs and higher discount rates. NVDA breaks below $190, triggering the structural-support stop.

wildcard.description: MATCH Act passes Congress, directly restricting ASML DUV/EUV exports to China — semis supply chain shock, ASML drops 15-20%, dragging the entire semis cluster. Alternatively, a US-China tech détente (Beijing-Washington summit) removes export-control overhang, sparking a broad semis re-rate led by ASML, INTC, and AXTI.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Deploy starter capital on NOK ACCUMULATE (R:R 2.5:1, wide-stop 1.53:1) and monitor NVDA for a $197.39 break with volume. No other entries — 8 of 28 names are hard-blocked on extension, and the PCE print keeps the rate-overhang intact. Wait for the July 2 NFP print before adding size.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously selective — one ACCUMULATE, several WATCH setups approaching entry zones
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 8 of 28 names are hard-blocked on technical extension — the semis cluster is the primary source, with memory names (MU, 000660.KS) and Intel/AMD/ASML all >14% above their 50-day SMAs. The PCE print at 3-year highs keeps rate-hike anxiety elevated, capping multiple expansion for the extended names and punishing duration-sensitive BigTech. One bright spot: NVDA's pullback to $195.74 (-6.8% below SMA50) with sizing R:R of 3.0:1 is the most actionable setup on the board once near-term resistance at $197.39 breaks.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Payrolls <150k, unemployment rises to 4.4%+ — labour market cooling reduces rate-hike odds, US10Y falls below 4.2%
bullish_upgrades: NVDA → BUY candidate (rate relief + volume reclaim)
D05_SI / O39_SI / U11_SI → WATCH candidate (US10Y breaks 4.20% NIM threshold)

bearish_impact: NVDA → remains WATCH (rate headwind caps upside)
MSFT → deeper CAUTION (duration-sensitive, capex costs rise)
BigTech cluster → remains blocked (AMZN, GOOG, PLTR stay under pressure)

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
